ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for clay-baked stuffed whole chicken (Beggar's Chicken). Mapped to broader 'chicken dish' class FOODON_03315116 as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Beggar's Chicken. A00GJ (cooked chicken meat) used as the closest defensible broader anchor. The dish includes stuffing (pork, mushrooms, pickled vegetables) and lotus leaf wrapping not captured by this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for Beggar's Chicken or sufficiently close traditional Chinese clay-baked poultry dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Beggar's Chicken is approximately 350 g, representing a generous individual portion of the whole clay-baked stuffed chicken with its aromatic stuffing.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No approved source contains a direct nutritional entry for Beggar's Chicken (Jiaohua Ji). Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for roasted chicken (meat and skin) as the primary protein-fat base, with adjustments for the stuffing components (pork, mushrooms, pickled vegetables) and sodium contribution from soy sauce and preserved vegetables. The input AI-generated values (protein 18.2 g, fat 12.5 g, carbohydrate 2.4 g, energy 195 kcal per 100 g) are broadly consistent with USDA roasted chicken with skin data and are retained as the best available estimate. Saturated fat estimated from USDA roasted chicken with skin data (~3.5 g/100 g). Sodium elevated to 380 mg/100 g reflecting marinade and pickled vegetable contribution, consistent with published data on marinated Chinese poultry dishes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Foster-Powell et al., peer-reviewed analogy for high-protein low-carbohydrate mixed meat dishes
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beggar's Chicken in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 20 (low) by analogy with high-protein, low-carbohydrate mixed meat dishes. The dish contains only 2.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g, predominantly from stuffing vegetables and trace starch. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (20 × 8.4) / 100 = 1.68, rounded to 1.7. Carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 2.4 × 3.5 = 8.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (380 mg/100 g; ~1330 mg per 350 g serving) from soy sauce marinade and pickled vegetables
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid adding further salt or soy sauce; a 350 g serving provides approximately 58% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (2.4 g/100 g) and low glycemic index (~20) with high protein supporting satiety
type2_diabetes.guidance: Beggar's Chicken is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate load and low GI; however, the moderate fat content (12.5 g/100 g) and high sodium should be considered in the overall meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.5 g/100 g from chicken skin and pork stuffing) and high sodium (380 mg/100 g) are the primary cardiovascular risk factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally and in moderate portions, removing chicken skin where possible to reduce saturated fat intake, and balancing the meal with low-sodium accompaniments.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beggar's Chicken (叫化鸡 / 叫化雞, Jiàohuā jī) is a celebrated traditional dish from Jiangsu Province, China, with origins dating back several centuries. A whole chicken is marinated in soy sauce, Shaoxing rice wine, ginger, scallions, and five-spice powder, then stuffed with a fragrant mixture of pork, shiitake mushrooms, and pickled vegetables. The stuffed chicken is wrapped tightly in large lotus leaves, which impart a distinctive earthy, floral aroma, and then encased in a thick shell of non-toxic clay before being slow-baked for four to six hours. The clay creates a sealed, steam-pressure environment that keeps the meat exceptionally moist and tender, allowing the flavours of the stuffing and lotus leaves to fully permeate the flesh. At the table, the clay shell is dramatically cracked open with a mallet, releasing aromatic steam. The resulting meat is falling-off-the-bone tender with a complex savoury flavour profile. Nutritionally, the dish is high in protein (18.2 g/100 g), moderate in fat (12.5 g/100 g), and very low in carbohydrates (2.4 g/100 g), making it suitable for low-carbohydrate dietary patterns. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 20 (low). Sodium content is elevated (380 mg/100 g) due to the marinade and pickled vegetables, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is a festive dish commonly served at banquets and family celebrations.

confidence_reason: No approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct entry for Beggar's Chicken. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for roasted chicken (meat and skin) as the primary protein-fat base, with adjustments for the stuffing components (pork, mushrooms, pickled vegetables) and sodium contribution from soy sauce and preserved vegetables. The AI-generated input values are broadly consistent with this adaptation and are retained. Confidence is medium due to the composite nature of the dish and absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, cholesterol, and moisture data for Beggar's Chicken are not available as a direct entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or any other approved source as of April 2026. The closest approved-source proxy (USDA roasted chicken, meat and skin) was used for core macros. Saturated fat estimated at 3.5 g/100 g from USDA roasted chicken with skin data (FDC ID 171477). Sodium of 380 mg/100 g reflects marinade and pickled vegetable contribution consistent with published data on marinated Chinese poultry preparations. The input record contained a duplicate portion entry (two identical '1 serving (350g)' entries); only one 350 g serving is used. The input source 'Journal of Ethnic Foods' is not on the approved source list and has not been used as primary evidence; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is used instead. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Beggar's Chicken exists in the University of Sydney GI Database.
